keyPoints: Certification of public keys involves verifying the identity of the key holder and ensuring the key is not compromised.
The certificate lifecycle includes issuance, deployment, revocation, and renewal.
Public-key management models include hierarchical, distributed, and hybrid approaches.
Alternative approaches to public-key management include web of trust and pre-shared key methods.
Key Exchange Protocols such as RSA and Diffie-Hellman are used for secure key exchange.
Digital Signatures are used for authentication and non-repudiation.
Certificate Authorities (CAs) issue digital certificates for public keys.
Certificate Revocation Lists (CRLs) and Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) are used for revocation.
